verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I TM protein with a large extracellular domain, resident primarily in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment and Golgi. Surface biotinylation on intact CD8 T cells detects a significant TMED10 pool at the plasma membrane, indicating the protein body does reach the outer leaflet. The protein cycles constitutively between ER/ERGIC/Golgi/PM via COPI and COPII vesicles; the PM pool is a documented minority fraction arising from normal trafficking, not from stress-induced or tissue-restricted translocation. This qualifies as dual_localization—the protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the surface via its own trafficking pathway, but the canonical compartment is intracellular endomembrane.
